g.text: Partial Write Buffer: Offset Tracking vs memmove (write_buf.c)
0x10 | size_t | write_offset // bytes already sent (head ptr)
Total: 32 bytes — fits in 1 cache line (64B)
Approach | Head Advance | Copy Cost | When to Use
Offset pointer | O(1) | zero | hot path — preferred
memmove compact | O(n) | O(pending)| when offset > capacity/2
Decision rule | always try | on append | reclaim wasted prefix
Flat Byte Array — Offset Pointer Approach (preferred, zero-copy)
memmove Compaction — Triggered on Append when offset > capacity/2
data[] heap alloc | 65536 B | 640 MB worst-case (all armed)
data[] typical | ~4096 B | ~40 MB (most bufs empty)
memmove cost | O(pending)| only when offset > cap/2
EPOLLOUT syscall | ~200 cyc | arm + disarm = 2 syscalls/burst
write() syscall | ~150 cyc | per flush iteration
TCP Ordering Invariant — Never Bypass the Queue
write_buf empty + new data | write() directly | fast path OK
write_buf non-empty + new data | append to buf ONLY | preserve order
EAGAIN from write() | append remainder | retry via EPOLLOUT
Violation: direct write bypasses| FORBIDDEN | TCP sees wrong order
State A: After first write() partial — n=3200 of 8192 sent
byte[0..3199] | SENT (gray) | already transmitted — space wasted
byte[3200] | ^ write_offset| ← next write() starts HERE
byte[3200..8191]| PENDING (blue)| 4992 bytes still to send
State B: After second EPOLLOUT — write(&data[offset], pending)
State C: Buffer drained — offset == write_len
byte[0..8191] | ALL SENT | buffer fully transmitted
EPOLLOUT deregistered via epoll_ctl(EPOLL_CTL_MOD, EPOLLIN only)
Invariant: EPOLLOUT MUST be removed or busy-loop occurs (100% CPU)
Trigger: new append() would fill capacity — must compact first
New data can now be appended without realloc
ARM EPOLLOUT: write() returns EAGAIN or short count
Trigger: write() == EAGAIN → send buffer full
Trigger: write() returned n < pending → partial write
epollout_armed = 1 // track state to prevent double-arm
If EPOLLOUT registered when write_buf is EMPTY:
send buffer has space → EPOLLOUT fires immediately
handler runs, no data to write, returns
Result: epoll_wait() returns every iteration → 100% CPU
Symptom: top shows 100% CPU, zero throughput
DISARM EPOLLOUT: write buffer fully drained
Action: epoll_ctl(MOD, fd, EPOLLIN) // remove EPOLLOUT
Rule: EPOLLOUT registered ONLY while write buffer non-empty

text.tspan: // Rule: if pending data exists, NEVER write directly
// (would violate TCP ordering — bypass queued bytes)
// EPOLLOUT already armed from previous backpressure
// Fast path: attempt direct write first

g.text: return 0; // all sent, EPOLLOUT stays disarmed

text.tspan: // Rule: if pending data exists, NEVER write directly
// (would violate TCP ordering — bypass queued bytes)
// EPOLLOUT already armed from previous backpressure
// Fast path: attempt direct write first

g.text: if (errno == EAGAIN) return 0; // wait for next EPOLLOUT

// write_offset += w; if offset==len → reset both to 0
